food.name: Extra Virgin Olive Oil

identity.basisReason: Per-100g as-sold basis matches oil labeling and avoids recipe dilution.

scoreReadiness.summary: Core oil nutrition is clean enough for launch scripting, but the entry still treats quality and polyphenol strength generically rather than tying them to a specific bottle or harvest profile.
blockers: polyphenol-rich reputation is real but not quantified in the current schema
authenticity and storage quality vary meaningfully across real-world extra virgin olive oils

sourceNotes: Identity locked to plain extra virgin olive oil, per 100 g as sold.
Core nutrient values align with USDA FoodData Central style extra virgin olive oil entries.
Polyphenol/quality discussion is intentionally treated as context rather than a locked nutrient metric.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: Vitamin e at 93% daily value, vitamin k at 50% daily value stand out most. That gives this food real vitamin support.
sections.subtitleText: Vitamin e at 93% daily value, vitamin k at 50% daily value stand out most. That gives this food real vitamin support.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: Minerals are basically not adding much here.
sections.subtitleText: Minerals are basically not adding much here.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: Pros first: polyphenol reputation is a real plus. It brings a widely recognised antioxidant angle beyond the visible macro profile. Contains useful polyphenols. Adds value beyond the core fat chemistry alone. Very simple ingredient identity. A clear wholefood-adjacent advantage over more industrial fat products.

sections.subtitleText: Pros first: polyphenol reputation is a real plus. It brings a widely recognised antioxidant angle beyond the visible macro profile. Contains useful polyphenols. Adds value beyond the core fat chemistry alone. Very simple ingredient identity. A clear wholefood-adjacent advantage over more industrial fat products.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: The drawbacks next: extremely energy dense. Still pure fat, so calories accumulate fast. Quality varies a lot. Source and authenticity matter in practice. Easy to overpour without noticing. A healthy image can make portion creep happen fast in practice.

narrationBlocks.text: 100g of fat. The fat profile is doing most of the work here. For this category, the real question is fat quality.
0g of carbs. Everything else is kind of whatever. Not relevant here.
0g of protein. Everything else here is pretty flat. Also not relevant here.
Vitamin e at 93% daily value, vitamin k at 50% daily value stand out most. That gives this food real vitamin support.
Minerals are basically not adding much here.

Pros first: polyphenol reputation is a real plus. It brings a widely recognised antioxidant angle beyond the visible macro profile. Contains useful polyphenols. Adds value beyond the core fat chemistry alone. Very simple ingredient identity. A clear wholefood-adjacent advantage over more industrial fat products.

The drawbacks next: extremely energy dense. Still pure fat, so calories accumulate fast. Quality varies a lot. Source and authenticity matter in practice. Easy to overpour without noticing. A healthy image can make portion creep happen fast in practice.
Big strengths are fat quality and vitamin e, but the biggest weaknesses are extremely energy dense and quality varies a lot.

explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while minerals are holding it back most. It lands in S tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in S tier with an overall score of 51.4. Section scores: fats 84.3, vitamins 56, minerals 0, pros 66.7, cons 50.
